title: Order decimals
description: Order 3 decimals with digits up to the thousandths from least to greatest.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the numbers from least to greatest.**  
*Put the lowest number on the left.*
  
[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: When we compare numbers, we should always start with comparing the largest place value.

Let's use a place value chart to compare all three numbers.

 Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ |$.$  | $\maroonD7$ | $0$ | $7$
$0$ |$.$  | $\maroonD0$ | $7$ | $4$
$0$ |$.$  | $\maroonD7$ | $7$ | $0$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

$0.\maroonD074$ has $\maroonD0$ tenths, so it is our smallest number. 

$0.\maroonD074$ $,$ ________ $,$ ________

Both $0.\maroonE707$ and $0.\maroonE77$ have $\maroonE{7}$ tenths, so we need to go to the next largest place value column, the hundredths.

$0.7\purpleE{0}7$ has $\purpleE0$ hundredths, so it is our next smallest number.

$0.074$$,$ $0.7\purpleE{0}7$ $,$  ______

$0.7\purpleE{7}$ has $\purpleE7$ hundredths, so it is our greatest number.

$0.074$ $,$ $0.707$$,$ $0.7\purpleE{7}$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$0.074$ $,$ $0.707$$,$ $0.77$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the numbers from least to greatest.**  
*Put the lowest number on the left.*

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: When we compare numbers, we should always start with comparing the largest place value.

Let's use a place value chart to compare all three numbers.

 Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1$ |$.$  | $\blueD9$ | $8$ | $0$
$1$ |$.$  | $\blueD0$ | $9$ | $8$
$1$ |$.$  | $\blueD8$ | $0$ | $9$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

$1.\blueD098$ has $\blueD0$ tenths, so it is our smallest number. 

$1.\blueD098$ $,$ ________ $,$ ________

$1.\blueD809$ has $\blueD8$ tenths, so it is our next smallest number. 

$1.098$ $,$ $1.\blueD809$ $,$ ________

$1.\blueD98$ has $9$ tenths, so it is our greatest number.

$1.098$ $,$ $1.809$ $,$ $1.\blueD98$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$1.098$ $,$ $1.809$ $,$ $1.98$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

$4.\blueD709$ has $\blueD7$ tenths, so it is our greatest number.

_______ $,$ ________ $,$ $4.\blueD709$

Both $4.\blueD097$ and $4.\blueD079$ have $\blueD0$ tenths, so we need to go to the next largest place value column, the hundredths.

$4.0\purpleC{7}9$ $,$ ________ $,$ $4.709$

$4.079$ $,$ $4.0\purpleC{9}7$ $,$ $4.709$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$4.079$ $,$ $4.097$ $,$ $4.709$

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: When we compare numbers, we should always start with comparing the largest place value.

Let's use a place value chart to compare all three numbers.

 Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueD1$ |$.$  | $0$ | $0$ | $1$
$\blueD0$ |$.$  | $1$ | $1$ | $3$
$\blueD1$ |$.$  | $1$ | $0$ | $1$

The greatest place value with different digits is the ones.

$\blueD0.113$ has $\blueD0$ ones, so it is our smallest number. 

$\blueD0.113$ $,$ ________ $,$ ________

Both $\blueD1.001$ and $\blueD1.101$ have $\blueD1$ one, so we need to go to the next largest place value column, the tenths.

$1.\purpleD001$ has $\purpleC0$ tenths, so it is our next smallest number.

$0.113$ $,$ $1.\purpleD001$ $,$ ________

$1.\purpleC{1}01$ has $\purpleC1$ tenth, so it is our greatest number.

$0.113$ $,$ $1.001$ $,$ $1.\purpleC{1}01$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$0.113$ $,$ $1.001$ $,$ $1.101$

The greatest place value with different digits is the ones.

$\blueD1.000$ has $\blueD1$ one, so it is our greatest number.

_______ $,$ ________ $,$ $\blueD1.000$

Both $\blueD0.35$ and $\blueD0.359$ have $\blueD0$ ones, so we need to go to the next largest place value column with different digits, the thousandths.

$0.35\purpleC0$ has $\purpleC0$ thousandths, so it is our smallest number.


$0.35\purpleC0$$,$ ________ $,$ $1.000$

$0.35\purpleC9$ has $\purpleC9$ thousandths, so it is our next smallest number.

$0.350$$,$ $0.35\purpleC9$$,$ $1.000$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$0.35$$,$ $0.359$$,$ $1$

Both $3.5\blueD50$ and $3.5\blueD54$ have $\blueD{5}$ hundredths, so we need to go to the next largest place value column, the thousandths.

$3.55\purpleC0$ has $\purpleC0$ thousandths, so it is our next smallest number.

$3.544$$,$ $3.55\purpleC0$$,$ ________ $,$

$3.55\purpleC4$ has $\purpleC4$ thousandths, so it is our greatest number.

$3.544$$,$ $3.550$$,$ $3.55\purpleC4$

The decimals in order from least to greatest:

$3.544$$,$ $3.55$$,$ $3.554$
